summary.intent: Upgrade the whatsapp-rust ecosystem from 0.2 to 0.5 to fix inbound WhatsApp messages being parsed as empty due to outdated protobuf definitions (GitHub #534).
summary.outcome: Successfully bumped 6 whatsapp crates to 0.5, implemented all new required trait methods in both store backends, fixed SKDM signature changes, added 12 new tests, and merged to a PR (#709) with all 4053 workspace tests passing.

repo: The whatsapp crate has two store backends that must be kept in parallel: sled_store.rs (sled-backed) and memory_store.rs (DashMap-backed).
whatsapp-rust must keep default-features = false to avoid sqlite-storage / libsqlite3-sys conflict.
wacore and wacore-binary 0.5 default features include 'simd' which requires unstable core::simd::Select — must disable defaults on both.
The pinned nightly toolchain is nightly-2025-11-30, used for fmt and the build.
The llama-cpp-sys-2 / CUDA build failure on macOS is a pre-existing environment issue, not introduced by this change; tests should be run without --all-features on macOS.
The project uses a 'beads' workflow for issue tracking alongside GitHub PRs.

code.finding: whatsapp-rust must use default-features = false (avoid sqlite) and explicitly list features including the new 'tokio-runtime' feature added in 0.5. wacore and wacore-binary also need default-features = false to avoid the simd/nightly issue.
whatsapp-rust 0.5 bot builder requires a 4th type parameter for the async runtime; must call .with_runtime(TokioRuntime) before .on_event().

wacore 0.5 adds 11 new required trait methods across SignalStore (get_max_prekey_id), AppSyncStore (get_latest_sync_key_id), and ProtocolStore (5 TC token methods + 3 sent message cache methods). SKDM methods changed String→Jid. Two new sled trees required for tc_tokens and sent_messages.
Parallel changes to sled_store.rs required: same 11 new trait methods, same SKDM String→Jid signature fix, two new DashMap fields for tc_tokens and sent_messages.
No changes were needed — waproto 0.5 kept the same core field names (conversation, extended_text_message, image_message, etc.).

workflow: cargo check -p <crate> is the efficient first step to surface all breaking API changes after a dependency bump before attempting fixes.
When Cargo.lock has merge conflicts, the cleanest resolution is to take main's version and re-run cargo fetch rather than manually resolving the lockfile.
Test SKDM-related code uses .parse() for Jid construction, not .into() from String.
Workspace tests should be run without --all-features on macOS to avoid CUDA-dependent crate build failures.

friction: wacore/wacore-binary 0.5 defaults enable a 'simd' feature requiring unstable core::simd::Select that the pinned nightly doesn't support — required discovering and disabling default-features on both crates.
The llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA dependency causes build failures under --all-features on macOS, making it impossible to use the standard 'just test' recipe directly.
Cargo.lock merge conflict with main required an extra merge commit after the PR was already created.
open_items: Users with existing WhatsApp pairings may need to re-scan their QR code if wacore struct serialization changed between 0.2 and 0.5 — should be documented in the PR description.
The 'just test' recipe may need updating to exclude CUDA-dependent crates on macOS to avoid pre-existing build failures masking real test regressions.
